mnemonics: CPU Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle: 'Freddy Eats Delicious Cake Every Day' (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Cycle)
CPU Components: 'ALU Loves Calculating, CU Loves Directing' (Arithmetic Logic Unit, Control Unit)
Instruction Cycle Stages: 'Fetch Decode Execute Store' - 'Friendly Dogs Enjoy Snacks'
keyPoints: The CPU is the brain of the computer, responsible for executing instructions
The fetch-decode-execute cycle is the fundamental process of instruction execution
Registers are small, high-speed memory locations within the CPU used for temporary storage
The Control Unit (CU) manages and coordinates the operations of the CPU
The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) performs mathematical calculations and logical operations
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the ALU and CU (ALU performs operations, CU directs operations)
Forgetting that the instruction cycle includes storing results after execution
Misunderstanding the purpose of registers (temporary storage within CPU, not main memory)
Assuming all instructions take the same amount of time to execute
Overlooking the importance of the system clock in synchronizing CPU operations
